# Changelog

All notable changes to supply-drop-bbs are documented here.
Versioning follows [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/) (pre-1.0:
minor bumps may carry breaking changes).

<!-- generated by git-cliff -->
## [0.9.5] — 2026-06-15

### Bug Fixes

- Make H show help in reading mode instead of exiting (#109) ([`5cb7511`](5cb751102445b89db2a9ccf40cac299d4aa31498))
- Run commands sent during room selection instead of dropping them (#108) ([`78953da`](78953dabc616650000272e64328bf90e8921e6b2))
- Make a re-sent bare `.` after posting idempotent (#107) ([`4894e97`](4894e974147c3db1a70c0458ea648b4d2bd4fd57))
- Merge "new messages" header into first frame so N can't strand it (#106) ([`211d1e8`](211d1e8f079d4b5a2ca7dc8c4d84dcdf8c11d064))
- Accept `-` sentinel for display name on mesh (#105) ([`9de40c4`](9de40c42ea106b6083dee06135c0277722e35292))
- Don't dedup the confirm password as a retransmission (#104) ([`446df4d`](446df4d3c7ce463457ee6daba8ca6f8589b0b170))

### Documentation

- Reconcile in-app help with USER_GUIDE command set (#110) ([`3972002`](3972002a95648986d7dbb216923d6e4a981996c8))

## [0.9.1] — 2026-06-14

### Bug Fixes

- Combine welcome banner and command response into one frame (#94) ([`c46c607`](c46c607e96b47bbb61ce7161e30cac2df86825e3))
- Record nodeinfo adverts from over-the-air packets ([`901bd5a`](901bd5a1b2eb60c3592b7907e2d6ffef27333a7a))
- Reconnect on unexpected reader task exit (SYN-38b) ([`a48f142`](a48f14283fc1c61004f6244d8b5144b15a3a88d4))
- Unify unauthenticated unknown-command response with help text ([`1b94568`](1b9456852e2764328af9ca050794bcbf97367788))
- Shorten unauthenticated unknown-command response to fit 126-char frame limit ([`29cc93e`](29cc93ec8092122ba3d66eb6be20accd589e87f7))
- Combine welcome banner and command response into one frame (#94) ([`a69f8ff`](a69f8ff9657220aff6c5edcbc46bafcc1eb8167a))
- Send welcome banner only on first contact, not every unauthenticated message ([`4721e24`](4721e2455abc8f7676ca394712c51cc90ac3944c))
- Record nodeinfo adverts from over-the-air packets ([`6b924de`](6b924de2edfe2580546c5023399f6aef92592723))
- Reconnect on unexpected reader task exit (SYN-38b) ([`2f6a162`](2f6a1624ff4d332b1b2835f55a20d05496895737))
- Apply CSRF origin check to the public login endpoint (SYN-5b) (#91) ([`5836433`](5836433be6c052030222fde1289ad23552d266c8))
- Default rate-limit config keys to 0 to suppress false-positive warning (SYN-3b) (#90) ([`543e4c3`](543e4c37659cb099e9445d267ecaeddfd4595d81))
- Add Referrer-Policy header to security headers middleware (SYN-9b) (#89) ([`a98c823`](a98c823fcb6bca3274a740d11dcaec2320bdc594))
- Update PermissionCtx field accesses to use accessor methods (SYN-4b) (#88) ([`5b58469`](5b584699c5022d39e5595cd81f94b0a53dc7ac19))
- Delegate parse_command to canonical Command::parse (SYN-45) (#65) ([`b807b11`](b807b11a5bdb98790195c8b9e2f59030f08c8c75))
- Prevent panic on truncated frame in strip_frame_header (SYN-36) (#68) ([`2b00cd7`](2b00cd76dff68e37a5b6585f74d1055596ab3db7))
- Make PermissionCtx fields private, add accessor methods (SYN-4) (#69) ([`8dc8d1b`](8dc8d1bc1b324fcc5fee618cd318c470d2d40eb9))
- Document unimplemented rate-limit keys and warn at startup (SYN-3) (#70) ([`e739f88`](e739f880c2ff88d21dacf6eaa2a45859aabef497))
- Add migration 0009 to rename case-collision orphan accounts (SYN-30) (#71) ([`9d146b5`](9d146b553be8eb375c01451c26f0c17cebc66935))
- Store full 32-byte pubkey in node credential table (SYN-11) (#72) ([`b075f38`](b075f380185eb28432db54be56da141f5a943c1a))
- Enforce Origin header check for CSRF and warn on non-loopback bind (SYN-5) (#74) ([`915f33e`](915f33ee0f5b8a719072adb10f03e0b8635fe935))
- Emit Content-Security-Policy and hardening headers on every response (SYN-9) (#75) ([`d34b4a2`](d34b4a2c54f70de52b2ebd8ba146467c6847db6c))
- Validate plugin name and command before spawning (SYN-10) (#76) ([`df18b3c`](df18b3c19247852018038d04f78da3efa9475eda))
- Fail hard on missing or unverifiable checksum (SYN-8) (#77) ([`3c1a439`](3c1a4392ee3be45e71fd3bcac046a31100a54b55))
- Guard reorder against self-reference (SYN-28) (#78) ([`92fcac6`](92fcac6620706ce65e0812e6aa140be6ea820690))
- Wrap message delete in transaction; warn on corrupt config (SYN-24, SYN-31) (#79) ([`5407d23`](5407d23b5f64a3edfe2f2129a1fdcebdda8fbdcd))
- Write config and zip backups atomically (SYN-25, SYN-26) (#80) ([`453c31c`](453c31c27097db047f2cb8bd99b28a5d115df4c2))

## [0.8.0] - 2026-05-27

### Added

- **Node key management CLI** — new `supply-drop-bbs node` subcommand for
  managing the companion device's private key without a running BBS:
  - `node show-key` — display the current node public key
  - `node export-key` — export the 64-char hex private key for backup before
    a firmware flash
  - `node import-key <hex>` — restore or migrate to a new key on a replacement
    device

- **Radio config in web admin Settings** — a new "Radio Parameters" section
  lets the sysop view and apply LoRa radio settings (frequency, bandwidth,
  spreading factor, coding rate, TX power) from the browser without a CLI.
  Region presets are shown with their parameter details; changes are sent to
  the device immediately via the live mesh connection.

- **Node identity panel in web admin** — the Settings page now shows the
  connected device's public key with a copy button, and provides an
  inline-editable import field to push a new private key to the device.

- **Radio parameter presets in setup wizard** — during initial configuration
  (`supply-drop-bbs setup`) the wizard now offers a named region preset
  selector for USB serial connections, with a "Custom…" option for manual
  LoRa parameter entry (frequency, bandwidth, spreading factor, coding rate,
  TX power). Previously only a simple preset index was stored.

- **TCP MeshCore as a setup wizard option** — the installer now offers
  TCP-bridged MeshCore (via `pymc_core`) alongside USB serial, so a Pi HAT
  on a separate host can be configured without any USB connection to the BBS
  server.

### Fixed

- **Pending key operations no longer hang on disconnect** — if a
  `node export-key` or `node import-key` command was in flight when the radio
  bridge disconnected, the caller would block indefinitely. The disconnect
  event now immediately resolves the pending operation with an error.

- **Device error frame unblocks pending key op** — an `InboundFrame::Err`
  from the device (e.g. unsupported command) now resolves any in-flight key
  operation with the error code rather than leaving the caller waiting.

- **`node import-key` returns correct HTTP status codes** — previously all
  import errors were mapped to `400 Bad Request`. Now bad key material returns
  400, transport unavailable returns 503, and other internal errors return 500.

- **Serial companion handshake robustness** — the `AppStart` handshake now
  discards unsolicited push frames (adverts, log data, etc.) that arrive
  before `SelfInfo`, instead of treating them as handshake failures. Fixes
  silent connection failures on busy mesh networks.

- **Contact list fetched on every connect** — the BBS now sends `GetContacts`
  immediately after a successful handshake so the contact list is always
  current after a reconnect, rather than waiting for the next advert cycle.

- **Non-UTF-8 node names no longer crash the session** — contact records
  containing malformed UTF-8 (e.g. a truncated multi-byte emoji) are now
  decoded with replacement characters (`from_utf8_lossy`) instead of
  returning a decode error that terminated the session and triggered an
  infinite reconnect loop.

- **Last Seen timestamps display correctly** — previously rendered as raw
  Unix epoch integers; now shown as human-readable relative times.

- **Setup wizard correctly reports config file ownership** — when run as
  root, the wizard chowns both the config file and its parent directory to
  the service user so that the web admin can atomically rewrite `config.toml`.
  Previously the directory chown result was discarded and ownership was
  reported as successful even when the directory chown failed, leaving the
  web admin unable to save config changes.

### Changed

- **Per-frame mesh log events demoted from INFO/DEBUG to DEBUG/TRACE** —
  high-frequency events (inbound message received, MsgWaiting fetch, command
  dispatch, reply send, per-frame rx/tx) were logged at INFO or DEBUG,
  producing thousands of lines per hour under normal load and filling disk
  rapidly during reconnect loops. Demoted to DEBUG (transport-level events)
  and TRACE (per-frame codec events); reconnect and session lifecycle events
  remain at INFO.

## [0.5.3] - 2026-05-12

### Added

- **Block reserved usernames at registration** — `bbs` and `system` are now
  rejected at registration (`Username::new` returns `InvalidUsername::Reserved`).
  System messages stored with those senders continue to round-trip correctly via
  the DB read path.

- **SSE-driven pending-users badge** — `/api/v1/sse/events` streams
  `user_created` and `user_validated` named events. The topbar badge now
  subscribes via `EventSource` for immediate updates, with a 120 s fallback
  poll.

- **In-session sysop password reset (`.PW <username>`)** — Sysops can now
  reset another user's password directly from any BBS session without needing
  shell or web-admin access. The command validates sysop permission and user
  existence, then prompts for the new password with confirmation.

- **Runtime log level toggle** — Changing the log level in web admin Settings
  now takes effect immediately without restarting the service. The
  `tracing_subscriber` filter is replaced in place via a reload handle; the new
  level is also persisted to config.toml.

## [0.5.2] - 2026-05-12

### Added

- **Pending-users indicator in topbar** — an orange "N pending" pill appears
  in the topbar on every page (and on mobile) whenever there are unvalidated
  accounts waiting for approval. Clicking it navigates directly to the Users
  page.

- **Plugins page section titles** — "built-in transports" and "process plugins"
  headings are now larger (1.2 rem) and use the theme accent colour.

## [0.5.1] - 2026-05-12

### Fixed

- **Mail privacy: registration notifications no longer visible to the new user** —
  Sysop DMs sent when a new user registers were being stored with the new user
  as the sender, so that user could read the notification (including the V/B
  commands) in their own Mail. The sender is now `bbs`, which is never a
  logged-in session, so only the recipient sysops see it.

- **Locked system room settings in web admin** — The edit modal for Mail, Aides,
  and Sysop rooms no longer shows the permission-level or read-only fields,
  since those settings are fixed for these rooms. The backend also rejects any
  PATCH request that attempts to change those fields for rooms 2–4. Description
  remains editable.

## [0.5.0] - 2026-05-12

### Added

- **`plugins.d/` drop-in directory for process plugin config** — plugin
  installers can now drop a `<name>.toml` file into
  `/etc/supply-drop-bbs/plugins.d/` instead of editing `config.toml` directly.
  Supply Drop merges all drop-in files at startup, and the files survive BBS
  upgrades and reconfiguration unconditionally (the setup wizard never touches
  `plugins.d/`).

  `supply-drop-bbs plugin add` now writes to `plugins.d/<name>.toml` and is
  safe to run idempotently on every plugin install or upgrade.
  `plugin remove` deletes the drop-in file. `plugin enable/disable` updates
  whichever file the plugin lives in. `plugin list` now shows a `SOURCE`
  column (`plugins.d` or `config.toml`).

  Legacy `[[plugins.process]]` entries in `config.toml` continue to work and
  are still managed by the web UI and CLI.

  The installer creates `/etc/supply-drop-bbs/plugins.d/` automatically.

### Fixed

- **Setup wizard preserves `[[plugins.process]]` entries on reconfigure** —
  the previous fix in v0.4.10 is superseded; with `plugins.d/` the wizard
  never needs to touch process plugin config at all.

## [0.4.10] - 2026-05-12

### Fixed

- **Setup wizard now preserves `[[plugins.process]]` entries on reconfigure** —
  running the installer (or `supply-drop-bbs setup`) on an existing installation
  and answering "Y" to reconfigure previously wrote a fresh config that silently
  dropped all process plugin entries added via `plugin add`. The wizard now reads
  and re-appends any existing `[[plugins.process]]` blocks verbatim, so
  reinstalling or reconfiguring the BBS no longer wipes externally-added plugins.

## [0.4.9] - 2026-05-12

### Fixed

- **Process plugin commands now work via external transports (e.g. telnet)** —
  `Command::parse` in `bbs-plugin-api` only recognised auth commands (`login`,
  `logout`, `register`, `help`). Any room navigation, message, or moderation
  command sent through a process-transport session was silently treated as
  `Unknown`. The full command set (`k`, `g`, `c`, `m`, `n`, `f`, `r`, `s`,
  `e`, `d`, `w`, `pending`, `v`, `b`, `ban`, `unban`, `u`, `whois`, `profile`,
  `passwd`, `.ff`, `.c`, `.dr`, `.er`, `.eu`, `.du`, etc.) is now handled.

- **`plugin add` is now idempotent** — re-running the plugin installer after a
  BBS upgrade no longer exits with an error when the plugin entry already
  exists in config. The command now prints an informational message and exits
  cleanly (exit 0), so installer scripts that run `plugin add` unconditionally
  on every upgrade continue to work correctly.

## [0.4.8] - 2026-05-12

### Fixed

- **Plugins page "settings" link now navigates correctly** — the link in the
  built-in transports config hint pointed to `/settings` (a full-page reload)
  instead of `/#/settings` (Vue Router hash route). Changed to
  `<router-link>` so it routes within the SPA without a page reload.

## [0.4.7] - 2026-05-12

### Fixed

- **Config write now preserves `[[plugins.process]]` sections** — toggling a
  native plugin (mesh/meshtastic/CLI) from the web UI or saving any setting
  via the Settings page previously serialised the config file with
  `toml::to_string()`, which could corrupt or drop `[[plugins.process]]`
  array-of-tables entries. Both handlers (`api_update_native_plugin` and
  `api_patch_config`) now use `toml_edit::DocumentMut`, the same
  format-preserving approach already used by `ProcessPluginManager`. Process
  plugin entries survive any web-UI config write intact.

## [0.4.6] - 2026-05-12

### Added

- **Sysop password reset — web UI and CLI** — sysops can now reset any
  user's password without requiring the old one. In the web admin, open
  the user detail drawer on the Users page and click **reset password**
  (sysop-only; minimum 6 characters; audit-logged as `reset_password`).
  From the CLI: `supply-drop-bbs user set-password <username>` prompts
  for a new password with hidden input and confirmation. The BBS does not
  need to be stopped for either path.
- **Native plugin visibility and toggle in web UI** — the Plugins page
  now shows a "built-in transports" section listing every compiled-in
  transport (MeshCore, Meshtastic, CLI) with its current enabled/disabled
  state. Sysops can toggle each transport on or off directly from the UI;
  a restart banner appears when a change requires a server restart. A
  **restart now** button posts to the existing `/api/v1/restart` endpoint.
- **`contrib/fake-host` test harness** — async Python script that spawns
  a process transport plugin, drives it interactively or from a JSONL
  scenario file, and colour-codes plugin↔host traffic. Simplifies
  transport plugin development without a running BBS.

### Fixed

- Corrected four inaccuracies in `docs/PROCESS_TRANSPORTS.md` reported
  during Telnet plugin development: wrong Rust type (`&HostMsg` →
  `&PluginMsg`), wrong message-direction examples, missing `text` field
  framing contract, and missing Telnet `hide_input` IAC note.
- Removed stale `[TBD]` placeholder from the `setup` subcommand docstring.
- Added `connection_type = "hat"` to the `[plugins.meshtastic]` section
  of `config.example.toml`.

## [0.4.5] - 2026-05-11

### Added

- **Meshtastic Pi HAT support** — new `connection_type = "hat"` variant in
  `[plugins.meshtastic]`. The setup wizard now offers a third connection option
  (USB / Pi HAT / TCP) and asks for the GPIO UART port (`/dev/ttyAMA0`,
  `/dev/serial0`, or custom). `install.sh` enables UART hardware and disables
  the serial console via `raspi-config` when the HAT type is detected, and adds
  the service user to the `dialout` group.
- **Web UI transport-aware navigation** — the sidebar "mesh / adverts" entry is
  now shown only when the MeshCore transport is enabled. A new
  `GET /api/v1/transports` endpoint (`{meshcore: bool, meshtastic: bool}`) lets
  the SPA query which built-in transports are active; call
  `WebPlugin::set_active_transports` before `start()` to wire it up.
- **`docs/TRANSPORT_PLUGINS.md` serial-port guidance** — new section 10a
  explains that USB port ordering is not guaranteed, documents the VID/PID
  labels used by the setup wizard, and covers UART path selection for
  Meshtastic Pi HAT setups.

### Changed

- **`prost` dep scoped to `bbs-meshtastic`** — moved from workspace
  `[workspace.dependencies]` to `crates/bbs-meshtastic/Cargo.toml`; no other
  crate uses protobuf.

## [0.4.4] - 2026-05-11

### Changed

- **Serial port selector now identifies radio chips** — the setup wizard
  labels each port by USB VID/PID: CP2102 shows as "Meshtastic/MeshCore
  radio — CP2102", CH340/CH9102 as "Meshtastic radio", ESP32-S3 native
  USB similarly, and the MeshCore HAT XR serial chip as "MeshCore HAT".
  Eliminates guessing between `ttyUSB0` and `ttyUSB1` when multiple
  devices are connected.

## [0.4.3] - 2026-05-11

### Fixed

- **Meshtastic serial handshake** — CP210x/CH34x chips assert DTR on port
  open which triggers a brief reset on Heltec V3 and T-Beam boards. The
  BBS now waits 2 seconds after opening the port before sending
  `WantConfig`, giving the firmware time to boot. Previously the
  handshake packet was lost and the radio sat silent for the entire
  session.

## [0.4.2] - 2026-05-11

### Changed

- **`transport-meshtastic` added to default features** — the Meshtastic
  transport is now compiled into every release binary. The setup wizard
  will ask which transports to enable (MeshCore, Meshtastic, or both)
  during installation without requiring a custom build.

## [0.4.1] - 2026-05-11

### Added

- **Meshtastic native transport** — full protobuf-over-serial/TCP codec
  implemented (`transport-meshtastic` feature). Supports direct USB serial
  connection (Heltec V3, T-Beam, RAK4631, etc.) and TCP to a running
  `meshtasticd` instance. Handles direct-message text packets, NodeInfo
  adverts, session lifecycle, deduplication, and reconnect with exponential
  backoff. Heartbeats sent every 30 seconds to keep the link alive.

## [0.4.0] - 2026-05-11

### Added

- **Multi-protocol transport architecture** — MeshCore and Meshtastic are now
  independent, co-equal transports. A single BBS instance can run MeshCore
  only, Meshtastic only, or both simultaneously.
- **`transport-meshtastic` feature flag** — new `bbs-meshtastic` crate wires
  the Meshtastic config, setup wizard, installer, and protobuf-over-serial/TCP
  radio codec.
- **`[plugins.meshtastic]` config section** — `connection_type` (`serial` or
  `tcp`), `serial_port`, `baud_rate`, `addr`, `command_prefix`, payload,
  reconnect, and delivery fields. Defaults to `enabled = false` so existing
  installs are unaffected.
- **`enabled` flag on `[plugins.mesh]`** — MeshCore transport can now be
  disabled without removing the config section. Defaults to `true` for
  backward compatibility.
- **Multi-transport setup wizard** — when both features are compiled in, the
  wizard presents a multi-select prompt to choose which protocols to configure.
  Each protocol has an independent connection config block.
- **Protocol-aware installer** — `install.sh` now parses both transport
  sections; pymc-companion is only installed when MeshCore HAT mode is active;
  a Meshtastic info block is shown when that transport is enabled.
- **Singleton-per-protocol enforcement** — each built-in protocol uses a single
  named TOML section (`[plugins.mesh]`, `[plugins.meshtastic]`), not an array,
  so the config parser itself prevents two instances of the same protocol.

## [0.3.0] - 2026-05-11

### Fixed

- **`migrate` command** — was a stub that exited 1; now opens the database and
  runs all pending sqlx migrations, exits 0 on success. Deployment scripts can
  sequence `migrate && run` for clean schema upgrades without starting the BBS.
- **`backup` command** — was a stub that exited 1; now triggers an immediate
  `VACUUM INTO` backup, creates the backup directory if absent, and prints the
  filename, size, and destination path.
- **Process plugin crash restart** — `restart_on_crash = true` in
  `[[plugins.process]]` now actually respawns the process after the configured
  `restart_delay_secs`. Previously the crash was logged but no restart occurred.

### Features

- **GPS location broadcast** - new `[location]` config section; when
  `latitude` and `longitude` are set the mesh transport sends
  `SetAdvertLatlon` to the radio on every connect so the node appears
  on the MeshCore map. Takes effect without a restart. The setup wizard
  prompts for coordinates during initial configuration.
- **Web UI config editor** - sysops can view and edit the config file
  from the admin web (`/settings`). Backend exposes `GET/PATCH
  /api/v1/config`; writes via `toml::Value` tree manipulation to
  preserve unknown sections. `[plugins.web] config_path` must point at
  the config file. The page shows the exact `chown`/`chmod` commands if
  the file is not writable.
- **Settings page UX** - timezone field uses browser `<datalist>` over
  all IANA names (defaults to server system timezone when unset);
  starting room is a dropdown populated from `/api/v1/rooms`; all
  fields validate before save with inline per-field error messages.
- **Help system restructure** - `H` shows a compact overview with
  single-letter topic shortcuts (`H M` mail, `H R` rooms, `H P` post,
  `H U` users, `H N` navigation, `H A` account). Each topic fits the
  156-byte mesh payload limit.
- **`node_location()` on `Host` trait** - default no-op implementation
  so existing plugins compile without changes. The mesh transport calls
  this after every connection to set the advert lat/lon.
- **Mesh America accreditation in setup wizard** - mission blurb and
  donation link printed after "Setup complete!".

## [0.2.0] - 2026-05-09

First tagged release. Covers all development since the initial skeleton
commit, including the full BBS feature set, admin web UI, LoRa mesh
transport, installer, and release tooling.

### Features

- **Plugin API** - trait-based plugin contract with `MockHost` for
  unit-testing plugins without a live host
  ([`1b4ed65`](1b4ed650c9803639fdf8bcdd916d055ccadbeef0))
- **SQLite persistence** - domain types (User, Room, Message) backed by
  SQLite with a versioned migration system
  ([`bd6731a`](bd6731a568aea205e4ecca60ed3a3e092ac9485f),
  [`8e5c9fd`](8e5c9fd26e320e53cf473fdd4eaa6cc5653ab0b7))
- **MeshCore transport** - TCP and native-serial connections to MeshCore
  radios; frame codec, session multiplexing, reconnect handling
  ([`d0eeaa3`](d0eeaa3f8a3221713ef901241b563621ad9493c5),
  [`1213192`](1213192bf748f841aadfc7311f9cceecfa79eec1))
- **CLI transport** - Unix domain socket transport for local terminal
  sessions ([`3a02580`](3a02580b2eef1d43193e78b3ddad98b48c2c6778))
- **Auth workflows** - register, login, and password-change workflows
  with validation and error feedback
  ([`53b8c1c`](53b8c1cbe67184f1fb9ccd4ed9d96cc78508512a))
- **Room system** - post, read-new (N), read-forward (F), read-reverse
  (R), read-single (S); public rooms and direct mail
  ([`21d3f0d`](21d3f0d9fba130ecfc7d9a786607c30a851698e1))
- **Moderation** - W/who, V/validate, B/ban, UNBAN, PENDING; validation
  required before posting; block list with message filtering
  ([`308ea9f`](308ea9fd9117b538723f1e4ee05a2781084877ec),
  [`abe72eb`](abe72eb900f8afab5524b3a430b2c016dc872eb8))
- **Admin web UI** - full-featured SPA: users, rooms, messages, reports,
  backups (zip, download, delete), sessions, log stream, settings, sysop
  login, green/purple themes
  ([`aa31d0b`](aa31d0b415d449b5370cca35a9b684e500f046fe),
  [`87a0daf`](87a0daffcc6cdb08f62cd833ed041a89044464d1),
  [`3cc9386`](3cc9386d0f6b4568e24c892b873e65940b7e5873))
- **One-command Pi installer** - `install.sh` configures systemd
  services, creates a data directory, and runs the Rust setup wizard
  ([`8772276`](8772276fc2bd01a56979943ad764b5f66e69b457))
- **Interactive setup wizard** - region/HAT selection, backup-dir
  prompt, pre-populated from existing config on re-run
  ([`d8b7521`](d8b75210f3d1ed68fc85902ba4c4b9265b862208),
  [`da13b2f`](da13b2fc3d0df1621d3d6f083ff84794baa6e271))
- **pymc-companion bridge** - radio bridge service for ZebraHat Pi HAT
  connection ([`875a8cd`](875a8cd878e1ed37d5da38ab31949460fe828a39))
- **Persistent mesh credentials** - auto-login for returning nodes using
  stored node public key
  ([`b70ae3c`](b70ae3c47dd97ac0a4c24a386eb64fb9e555e33c))
- **Auth-aware help** - unauthenticated users see only commands available
  to them ([`ba03c13`](ba03c1335883f71d5967d0eb6a3ccbd76469c5e0))
- **Analytics reports** - top senders, top rooms, daily volume chart,
  stale rooms ([`30301756`](30301756f5874030b4daeb833735ee8c72b4e6b3))
- **Command parity with mesh-citadel** - case-insensitive commands, `h`
  alias, categorized help, `H all` to list everything
  ([`c70a7fc`](c70a7fc50694425db6d05bc8d25707c23568b54a),
  [`64987a2`](64987a29495d7368f793ca6df4aa60424622f183))
- **Sysop/System rooms** - seeded on first run; protected from deletion
  ([`30301756`](30301756f5874030b4daeb833735ee8c72b4e6b3),
  [`935fdce`](935fdceb349012eec2a3344e38781f8727570f0e))
- **HTTP log polling** - admin log stream switches to reliable
  sequence-cursor polling; `CommandExecuted` event captures all BBS
  activity ([`943e9ce`](943e9ce8bdaf4a346e02db1ab8e1ff72d4434599))
- **Zip backups** - DB + config bundled in a single `.zip` archive;
  download and delete from web UI
  ([`c075296`](c075296dfd683ef0f9596c566bdfdf9f0439abee),
  [`a47e6f2`](a47e6f2e4df2e2e9ec916be99f82f0aa953033de))
- **Supply-drop SVG icon** - new transparent icon used in nav header and
  favicon ([`eef0bef`](eef0befafd26b61404751170ff98324c9679c44b))
- **Release tooling** - `git-cliff` changelog generation, `cargo-release`
  version management, GitHub Actions release workflow with cross-compiled
  binaries for `aarch64`, `armv7`, and `x86_64`

### Bug Fixes

- Security: cross-room message delete, login hardening, block filtering
  wired into all read paths (N/F/R/S)
  ([`a295085`](a295085a4550b08859b0f142b5e5001bea8c6660))
- Stale mesh sessions recovered correctly after server restart
  ([`046601433`](046601433a49a011602b3208d8bfb6fb071d0ede))
- Dedup window reduced 60 s → 10 s to avoid silently dropping `h`/`help`
  commands after failed login attempts
  ([`a47e6f2`](a47e6f2e4df2e2e9ec916be99f82f0aa953033de))
- Unvalidated users now have their permission level refreshed from the DB
  on every command (no stale level after sysop validates)
  ([`2b0e4c4`](2b0e4c4a6c5c177dbfb35321b22a7564727441be))
- Stale queued messages drained on radio bridge reconnect
  ([`f11e411`](f11e411bef6aec03b9fbbff19e6223f23933e8ec))
- Charts always render in reports page; grid and axes visible even with
  no data ([`c61d0b7`](c61d0b7d5f93bfe4e7f5f9cbe84228344876607e))
- Embedded newlines collapsed in mesh message listings
  ([`9ca6fdd`](9ca6fdd4fb0867f4489c3cd50bc5000e0ec2f5fd))
- Sessions panel in web UI auto-refreshes every 10 s
  ([`a47e6f2`](a47e6f2e4df2e2e9ec916be99f82f0aa953033de))
- Migration 0002 reverted to original 3-room seed; 0004 adds Sysop/System
  rooms as a safe append (migrations are now append-only)
  ([`c53216c`](c53216c646b85bb37a377867746fe750160d0802))

### Documentation

- `ARCHITECTURE.md`, 13 ADRs, `PLUGIN_API`, `CONFIG`, `OPERATIONS`,
  `PROTOCOL` stubs
- Full CLI reference and lost-sysop recovery instructions
  ([`b26d6bf`](b26d6bfab605e04116cfd1b337dc74415dc7072c))
- `CLAUDE.md` - pre-commit checklist, commit conventions, migration rules
